description: Calm, peaceful, and untroubled. 'Thiro' names a stillness that shows: a serene person or scene settles the people around it. It is stronger than 'shena' (calm).
sound_symbolism: The word radiates stillness. 'Thi' /θi/ begins with breath held still, the serene pause. Then 'ro' opens with quiet radiance, peace glowing outward.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'thiro norawhi' (serene dusk). More elevated than 'shena' (calm)—implies a quality that extends beyond the self to affect surroundings.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Serenity is calm that has deepened into radiance—the peaceful presence that naturally calms others.
pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Serene beauty was Art Nouveau's highest aim—grace that soothes rather than excites.
